test_completeness.justification: Five tablet-view ACs (FR-01.38). Pure client UI; every testable behaviour is unit- or E2E-tested. The two device-only outcomes (the visual safe-area inset and the real one-finger touch-pan) are untestable here because env(safe-area-inset-bottom) resolves to 0 in headless and synthetic touch bypasses touch-action gesture arbitration.

behaviors.behavior: AC-1: in the compact band an expanded sidebar can be collapsed back to the rail (bidirectional); desktop shows no toggle
behaviors.evidence: client/src/components/sidebar/SidebarNav.test.tsx 'compact band: expand then collapse back to the rail' + 'desktop: no collapse control'; e2e/flows/80-tablet-responsive.spec.ts 'sidebar collapse is bidirectional' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: AC-2: the board carousel reveals the last (Done) column in full at tablet width (no hard clip)
behaviors.evidence: e2e/flows/80-tablet-responsive.spec.ts 'board carousel reveals the last (Done) column FULLY' toBeInViewport({ratio:1}) PASSED

behaviors.behavior: AC-3: the content scroll container reserves env(safe-area-inset-bottom)
behaviors.evidence: client/src/layouts/MainLayout.test.tsx 'content scroll container reserves the bottom safe-area inset' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: AC-3: bottom content visually clears the device safe-area inset on an inset-bearing device
behaviors.reason: env(safe-area-inset-bottom) resolves to 0px in jsdom and headless Chromium; the inset only renders on a notched/home-indicator device. Sven real-device smoke.

behaviors.behavior: AC-4: the list-view Title is the widest column at small resolutions
behaviors.evidence: client/src/components/external/TaskList.test.tsx 'title column is greedy (w-full)'; e2e/flows/80-tablet-responsive.spec.ts 'list view: Title is the widest column' (real bounding-box widths) PASSED

behaviors.behavior: AC-5: attachTouchScroll reserves the gesture (touch-action:none) on attach and restores it on dispose
behaviors.evidence: client/src/components/terminal/touch-scroll.test.ts 'reserves the gesture by setting touch-action:none ... and restores it on dispose' PASSED

behaviors.behavior: AC-5: a one-finger pan actually scrolls the terminal on a real touch device
behaviors.reason: synthetic TouchEvents (jsdom unit + Playwright) bypass the browser's touch-action gesture arbitration, which is the exact mechanism under test; only a real touch device exercises it. Sven real-device smoke.

surface_verification.justification: Spec 80 (tablet-responsive, chromium) executed against an isolated worktree stack (Hono :3848 + Vite :5174, SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE=local): 20/20 passed, exit 0. Includes the three new ACs (AC-1 bidirectional collapse with real width transitions, AC-2 Done-column ratio:1 reveal, AC-4 real column-width measurement) plus the desktop non-regression set.
